zh.lifeSummary: 當John Cloyes於1604年出生在英國艾可可爾斯特時，他的父親Peter Cloyes37歲，母親Elizabeth Grace Peekes33歲。他於1635年10月12日與Abigail Mournings在英國素福爾可武爾福斯東結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1676年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼波藍德法爾姆斯享年72歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Cloyes于1604年出生在英国艾可可爾斯特时，他的父亲Peter Cloyes37岁，母亲Elizabeth Grace Peekes33岁。他于1635年10月12日与Abigail Mournings在英国素福爾可武爾福斯東结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1676年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼波藍德法爾姆斯享年72岁。

nameMeanings.name: Cloyes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English clouse, cluse, Old English clūse ‘bar, enclosure, narrow pass’, later ‘mill dam, sluicegate, or floodgate’. In the latter sense it may have been a metonymic occupational name applied to a miller. See also Clowser .
English: variant of Close or Clow , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Clow
meanings: English (East Midlands and East Anglia): probably from Middle English cloue, a false singular form of Middle English clouse ‘bar, enclosure, narrow pass’, later ‘mill dam, sluice-gate, floodgate’. See Clowes .
English and Scottish (southern and central Scotland): variant of   Clough   1.
English (Devon): from an earlier form of modern Cornish cleath ‘dyke, earthwork’, as found in the placename Clovelly (Devon) and most likely a habitational name referring to that place.

variants.name: Clews
meanings: English (West Midlands and Cheshire): variant of Clowes or Clew, itself a variant of Clough or Clow .

variants.name: Clowser
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by a clowse, Middle English clouse, cluse, Old English clūse ‘bar, enclosure, narrow pass’, later ‘mill dam, sluice-gate, floodgate’. Clowser is a type of formation (topographic term + -er) that is especially characteristic of Sussex and the adjacent counties. It was synonymous with atte Clouse, and the Clowser family is probably descended from Thomas atte Cluse, recorded in 1332 in Warnham, Sussex.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

Historical Boundaries 1630: Maine, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1668: Massachusetts Claim, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1691: Massachusetts, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1716: York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1760: Cumberland, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1776: Cumberland, Massachusetts, United States 1820: Cumberland, Maine, United States

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When John Cloyes was born in 1604, in Colchester, Essex, England, his father, Peter Cloyes, was 37 and his mother, Elizabeth Grace Peekes, was 33. He married Abigail Mournings on 12 October 1635, in Woolverstone, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died in 1676, in Falmouth, Cumberland, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 72.

ko.lifeSummary: John Cloyes(이)가 1604년 영국 에세스 코체스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Peter Cloyes(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Grace Peekes(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1635년 10월 12일 영국 수폴크 보버스톤에서 Abigail Mournings(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1676년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 쿠벌랜드 파모우스에서 72세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Cloyes nasceu em 1604, em Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Peter Cloyes, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Grace Peekes, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Abigail Mournings em 12 de outubro de 1635, em Woolverstone, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1676, em Falmouth, Cumberland, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 72 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Cloyes родился в 1604 г. в местности колчестир, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Peter Cloyes было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Grace Peekes, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Abigail Mournings 12 октября 1635 г. в местности вулвирстоун, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер в 1676 г. в местности фалмоут, кумбирланд, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 72 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Cloyes nació en 1604, en Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Peter Cloyes, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Grace Peekes, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Abigail Mournings el 12 de octubre de 1635, en Woolverstone, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1676, en Falmouth, Cumberland, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 72 años.
